title: Hologic Selenia Series Mammography System - Detector calibration or flat panel artifacts
description: Identifies external causes of detector artifacts or calibration failures, including contamination, positioning, and system setup issues affecting image quality.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is in the system and no exposure is active. Why: Prevents unnecessary radiation exposure during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Identify Artifact Type and Consistency
steps.instructions: Review images and determine: Lines, bands, or pixel dropouts; Shading or non-uniform brightness; Fixed vs. intermittent artifacts. Expected Outcome: Fixed artifacts → likely detector or calibration issue; Intermittent artifacts → possible connection or environmental issue.

steps.title: Verify Artifact Across All Displays and Images
steps.instructions: Check: Acquisition workstation monitor; Review workstation (if available); Stored PACS images. Expected Outcome: Artifact only on one display → monitor issue; Artifact on all images → detector/system issue.

steps.title: Inspect Detector Surface and Compression Paddle
steps.instructions: Check for: Dust, debris, or contrast residue; Fingerprints or fluid contamination; Damaged or dirty compression paddle. Clean using approved wipes if needed. Expected Outcome: Contamination removed may eliminate image artifacts. Why: Even small debris can create visible artifacts in mammography imaging.

steps.title: Verify Proper Detector Positioning and Compression Setup
steps.instructions: Ensure: Paddle is properly seated; No tilt or uneven compression; Detector is fully engaged in position. Expected Outcome: Improper positioning corrected may resolve shading or calibration errors.

steps.title: Perform Detector Calibration / Flat Field Correction
steps.instructions: Run the system’s detector calibration (flat field) procedure per user-accessible controls. Expected Outcome: Successful calibration removes non-uniformity or fixed pattern noise; System completes calibration without errors. Why: Calibration compensates for detector sensitivity variations.

steps.title: Check for Environmental or Interference Factors
steps.instructions: Assess for: Recent room changes (equipment moved, shielding changes); Nearby electrical interference sources; Temperature or humidity extremes. Expected Outcome: Environmental correction stabilizes detector response if affected.

steps.title: Inspect External Cables and Connections (If Accessible)
steps.instructions: Check visible detector-related cabling (if applicable on system configuration): Secure connections; No pinched or damaged cables. Expected Outcome: Restoring connection integrity may resolve intermittent artifacts.

steps.title: Reboot the System
steps.instructions: Perform a controlled shutdown and restart. Expected Outcome: Temporary detector communication or calibration faults may clear after reboot.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported vertical line artifacts present on all mammography images.
CCR.Cause: Detector calibration drift and contamination identified on detector surface.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned detector surface and performed flat field calibration; artifacts resolved and image quality restored.

helpfulDetails: Appears on all images or intermittent

title: Hologic Selenia Series Mammography System - X-ray generator or exposure faults
description: Identifies external causes of exposure failures such as interlocks, power issues, positioning, or readiness conditions preventing X-ray generation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is in compression and no exposure is active. If a patient is present, safely release compression and move them to another unit. Prevents unintended radiation exposure and ensures safe troubleshooting conditions.

steps.title: Verify System Ready Status
steps.instructions: Check the system interface for “Ready” or equivalent status. Look for warning messages or system faults. System indicates ready for exposure. The generator will not fire unless all system conditions are satisfied.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop (E-Stop) Buttons
steps.instructions: Inspect all E-stop buttons (room, gantry, console). Ensure they are fully released. Releasing E-stop clears interlock faults and allows exposure. Engaged E-stops disable generator output.

steps.title: Inspect Door and Room Interlocks
steps.instructions: Verify the exam room door is fully closed and latched. Check any interlock indicators on the system. Closed door clears exposure inhibit condition. Open doors or interlock faults prevent X-ray generation for safety.

steps.title: Verify Compression and Paddle Positioning
steps.instructions: Ensure the compression paddle is properly attached and engaged. Confirm adequate compression is applied if required by the system. Proper positioning allows exposure enablement. Many systems require correct compression and positioning before enabling exposure.

steps.title: Confirm Technique and Exam Setup
steps.instructions: Review selected exam type, technique factors, and detector readiness. Ensure no invalid settings or incomplete setup steps exist. Valid technique settings allow exposure attempt. Incorrect or incomplete exam setup can block generator activation.

steps.title: Check System Power Stability
steps.instructions: Verify power to the system including main indicators and generator cabinet. Confirm no recent interruptions and check UPS if available. Stable power with no interruptions. Generator faults often occur with unstable or insufficient power.

steps.title: Inspect Cables and Connections (External Only)
steps.instructions: Check visible cables between generator cabinet, detector, and system console. Look for loose, damaged, or disconnected cables and reseat if necessary. Secure connections restore communication if fault was connection-related.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down the system using proper procedure. Wait 2–3 minutes, then power back on. Transient generator or interlock faults may clear. Resets system synchronization and clears temporary faults.

steps.title: Attempt Test Exposure (No Patient)
steps.instructions: Perform a system test exposure if allowed by protocol. Successful exposure confirms resolution. If resolved, return system to service.

CCR.Complaint: System would not allow exposure; “generator fault” displayed during exam setup.
CCR.Cause: Room door interlock not fully engaged, preventing exposure enablement.
CCR.Resolution: Secured door latch, verified system ready status, and confirmed successful test exposure.

helpfulDetails: Final system status (returned to service or removed)

title: Hologic Selenia Series Mammography System - Compression Paddle Sensor or Positioning Errors
description: Identifies external causes of compression paddle sensor or positioning errors preventing proper compression, including alignment, obstructions, or accessory issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is in compression and the paddle is fully released. If a patient is present, safely remove them and move to another unit. Why: Prevents injury from unintended compression movement.

steps.title: Observe Error Message and System Behavior
steps.instructions: Note specific error messages related to paddle position or sensor faults. Attempt small paddle movements using controls. Expected Outcome: No movement → possible interlock or sensor issue. Partial/intermittent movement → alignment or obstruction issue. Why: Helps determine if the issue is mechanical, sensor-related, or control-related.

steps.title: Verify Paddle Installation and Type
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the compression paddle. Ensure it is fully seated and locked into place and correct paddle type for the exam. Expected Outcome: Properly seated paddle clears positioning errors. Why: Misaligned or incompatible paddles can prevent sensor recognition.

steps.title: Inspect Paddle Mount and Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Check the paddle holder and sensor interface for debris or dust, bent or damaged mounting hardware, and misalignment. Clean if needed using approved methods. Expected Outcome: Clearing contamination or correcting alignment restores sensor detection. Why: Sensors rely on proper physical alignment and clean contact surfaces.

steps.title: Check for Physical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Inspect around the compression assembly for cables, tubing, accessories, positioning aids, or foreign objects in the track or mechanism. Remove any obstruction. Expected Outcome: Paddle moves freely without restriction. Why: Obstructions can trigger position errors or prevent full travel.

steps.title: Verify Compression Motion and Limits
steps.instructions: Attempt full range compression movement without a patient and observe for smooth travel, sudden stops, or inconsistent position readings. Expected Outcome: Smooth, consistent motion with accurate position feedback. Why: Irregular motion may indicate sensor misreads or mechanical binding.

steps.title: Perform System Reset / Reinitialization
steps.instructions: Restart the system using proper shutdown procedure. Expected Outcome: Temporary sensor or positioning faults may clear after reboot. Why: Resets system communication and reinitializes position sensors.

steps.title: Check Interlocks and System Readiness
steps.instructions: Verify all system interlocks are satisfied including doors closed, no active faults, and system shows Ready status. Expected Outcome: System allows normal paddle movement and positioning. Why: Interlocks can disable motion and mimic sensor faults.

CCR.Complaint: Compression paddle not recognized and unable to position for exam.
CCR.Cause: Compression paddle not fully seated and debris present in paddle sensor interface.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated paddle, cleaned sensor area, and verified normal compression operation.

helpfulDetails: Final status: Fully operational or removed from service

title: Hologic Selenia Series Mammography System - Gantry motion or positioning faults
description: Identifies external causes of gantry motion or positioning faults, including obstructions, interlocks, control issues, or system readiness conditions preventing movement.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is positioned in the unit and the gantry is not in compression. If a patient is present, safely remove them and move to another unit. Why: Prevents injury from unintended gantry movement.

steps.title: Observe Error Message and Motion Behavior
steps.instructions: Attempt a small gantry movement and note: No movement at all, Stops immediately, Moves intermittently or inaccurately. Expected Outcome: No movement → likely interlock or control issue. Partial/intermittent movement → possible obstruction or encoder issue. Why: Helps determine whether the issue is mechanical, electrical, or control-related.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop (E-Stop) Buttons
steps.instructions: Inspect all E-stop buttons (gantry, room, operator console). Ensure they are fully released. Expected Outcome: Releasing an engaged E-stop restores gantry motion. Why: E-stops disable all motion functions for safety.

steps.title: Verify Compression Paddle Status
steps.instructions: Ensure the compression paddle is: Fully released, Properly seated, Not obstructing gantry travel. Expected Outcome: Proper paddle positioning allows normal gantry movement. Why: Paddle position can trigger motion interlocks.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Obstructions
steps.instructions: Check around and behind the gantry for: Cables or cords, Step stools or positioning aids, Wall or detector clearance issues. Remove any obstruction. Expected Outcome: Clearing obstructions restores normal movement. Why: Mechanical resistance can trigger safety stops or positioning faults.

steps.title: Verify System Ready / Exam State
steps.instructions: Check the system interface for readiness status. Ensure: No active exam is locking motion, No system warnings preventing movement. Expected Outcome: System shows ready or idle state with motion enabled. Why: Certain states prevent gantry movement for safety or workflow control.

steps.title: Test Gantry Controls (All Input Methods)
steps.instructions: Test gantry movement using: Control panel, Footswitch (if applicable), Hand controls. Expected Outcome: Movement should respond consistently across all controls. Why: Helps identify if the issue is input/control-related rather than mechanical.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down the system using proper procedure. Wait 2–3 minutes, then power back on. Expected Outcome: Temporary faults or positioning sync errors may clear after reboot. Why: Motion systems rely on proper initialization and position referencing.

steps.title: Listen for Unusual Sounds During Movement Attempt
steps.instructions: Attempt movement and listen for: Clicking or grinding, Motor engagement without motion, No sound at all. Expected Outcome: Motor sound with no movement → possible mechanical binding. No sound → possible interlock or control issue. Why: Provides clues about motor vs control failure.

CCR.Complaint: Gantry would not move; positioning error displayed during exam setup.
CCR.Cause: No external obstruction found; E-stop released; system restart did not restore motion, indicating likely internal gantry drive issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service, labeled Out of Service, and escalated for vendor repair.

title: Hologic Selenia Series Mammography System - DICOM / PACS transfer failures
description: Identifies external causes of DICOM/PACS transfer failures such as network issues, configuration errors, or server availability preventing image transmission.

steps.title: Ensure Patient and Data Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm no active exam is in progress and all acquired images are saved locally. If needed, move patient workflow to another unit. Why: Prevents data loss and workflow disruption during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Network Connection Status
steps.instructions: Check network indicators on the acquisition workstation: Network icon status (connected vs disconnected), Link lights on Ethernet ports. Expected Outcome: System shows active network connection. Why: Loss of network connectivity is the most common cause of DICOM transfer failure.

steps.title: Inspect Ethernet and Network Cabling
steps.instructions: Check all visible network cables between: Workstation and wall port, System cabinets and switches. Reseat cables and inspect for damage. Expected Outcome: Secure connections restore communication if cabling was loose. Why: Physical layer issues often cause intermittent or complete transfer failures.

steps.title: Test Network Port / Wall Connection
steps.instructions: If possible, connect to a known working network port or test the port using another device (laptop). Expected Outcome: Verified active network port. Why: Rules out facility network drop or switch port failure.

steps.title: Confirm PACS Connectivity and Availability
steps.instructions: Check if other modalities can send images to PACS. Contact IT or PACS admin if needed. Expected Outcome: PACS is reachable and accepting studies. Why: If PACS is down or rejecting studies, the issue is not with the modality.

steps.title: Verify DICOM Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Review DICOM settings on the workstation: AE Title, IP address, Port number, Destination configuration. Compare against known correct configuration. Expected Outcome: Settings match PACS configuration. Why: Incorrect configuration prevents successful association with PACS.

steps.title: Attempt Manual Resend of Images
steps.instructions: Select a stored study and manually resend to PACS. Expected Outcome: Successful transmission confirms restored communication. Why: Verifies whether the issue is persistent or intermittent.

steps.title: Check for Error Messages or Queue Backlog
steps.instructions: Review system message logs or DICOM queue: Stuck or failed studies, Repeated transmission errors. Clear or retry failed jobs if appropriate. Expected Outcome: Queue clears and studies transmit successfully. Why: Backlogged or failed jobs can block new transmissions.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down the system properly, wait 2–3 minutes, and power back on. Expected Outcome: Temporary communication or software issues are resolved. Why: Restart resets network services and DICOM processes.

CCR.Complaint: Mammography unit unable to send images to PACS; studies stuck in queue.
CCR.Cause: Loose Ethernet connection at workstation causing intermittent network loss.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated network cable, verified connectivity, and successfully resent queued studies.

helpfulDetails: Final system status (operational or removed from service)

title: Hologic Dimensions Series Mammography System - Detector Calibration or Tomosynthesis Artifact Issues
description: Identifies external causes of detector artifacts or tomosynthesis image issues including contamination, calibration state, positioning, and environmental factors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is in compression and no exposure is active. If needed, move the patient to another unit.

steps.title: Identify Artifact Type and Pattern
steps.instructions: Review recent images and determine lines, bands, or pixel dropouts, shading or brightness non-uniformity, artifacts only in tomosynthesis vs 2D, and fixed vs intermittent appearance.

steps.title: Verify Artifact Across Displays and Systems
steps.instructions: Check images on acquisition workstation, review workstation (if available), and PACS to determine if artifact is display-related or system-related.

steps.title: Inspect Detector Surface and Compression Paddle
steps.instructions: Check for dust, debris, contrast residue, fingerprints, fluid contamination, and damaged or dirty compression paddles. Clean using approved wipes if needed.

steps.title: Verify Paddle Type and Positioning
steps.instructions: Ensure correct paddle type is installed, properly seated and aligned, and no tilt or uneven compression surface is present.

steps.title: Check Calibration Status
steps.instructions: Review for calibration due or expired alerts, recent calibration failures, and flat-field or offset calibration status. Run calibration if appropriate and allowed.

steps.title: Evaluate Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Check for temperature or humidity outside normal range, recent system warm-up or startup, and nearby equipment causing vibration or interference.

steps.title: Perform System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down using proper procedure, wait 2–3 minutes, then restart.

steps.title: Check for Recent Changes or Service Activity
steps.instructions: Determine if detector was recently serviced or replaced, software updates were performed, or calibration was interrupted previously.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported persistent vertical line artifacts on tomosynthesis images.
CCR.Cause: Contamination and incomplete detector calibration identified during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Detector surface cleaned and calibration performed; artifacts resolved and system returned to service.

title: Hologic Dimensions Series Mammography System - X-ray generator or exposure synchronization faults
description: Identifies external causes of exposure failures or synchronization faults preventing X-ray generation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is in compression and no exposure is active. If needed, move the patient to another unit.

steps.title: Verify System “Ready” Status
steps.instructions: Check the system interface for “Ready” or equivalent status and look for any warning messages or fault indicators.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop (E-Stop) Buttons
steps.instructions: Inspect all E-stop buttons (gantry, room, operator console) and ensure they are fully released.

steps.title: Verify Room and Door Interlocks
steps.instructions: Ensure the exam room door is fully closed and interlock indicators are normal.

steps.title: Inspect Compression and Positioning Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the compression paddle is properly installed and compression is within acceptable range. Verify the breast support and detector are properly aligned.

steps.title: Check Footswitch / Exposure Switch Operation
steps.instructions: Test the exposure switch or foot pedal. Ensure it is connected, confirm no physical damage, and try an alternate switch if available.

steps.title: Review System Error Messages and Logs
steps.instructions: Document any generator or synchronization error codes displayed.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled System Restart
steps.instructions: Shut down the system using proper procedure, wait 2–3 minutes, then power back on.

steps.title: Verify Power Stability to the System
steps.instructions: Check that the system has stable power with no interruptions, proper connection to power source, and no visible power alarms.

steps.title: Confirm Generator Warm-Up / Calibration Status (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Check if the system requires warm-up or calibration before exposure.

CCR.Complaint: System would not initiate exposure; generator fault displayed during exam.
CCR.Cause: Exposure footswitch found disconnected, preventing trigger signal to generator.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected footswitch and verified normal exposure operation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or escalated)

title: Hologic Dimensions Series Mammography System - Compression paddle sensor or force calibration errors
description: Identifies external causes of paddle sensor or force calibration errors, including misalignment, incorrect paddles, obstructions, or system readiness issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no patient is in compression and the paddle is fully released. If necessary, move the patient to another unit.

steps.title: Observe Error Message and Paddle Behavior
steps.instructions: Note the exact error message and attempt small paddle movements using controls. No movement indicates possible interlock or recognition issue. Movement with error indicates possible calibration or sensor issue.

steps.title: Verify Compression Paddle Installation
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the compression paddle. Ensure it is fully seated and locked, correct paddle type for the exam, and proper orientation.

steps.title: Inspect Paddle and Mounting Interface
steps.instructions: Check for debris, damage, or wear on paddle, bent or damaged mounting brackets, and obstruction at docking interface. Clean or remove any visible obstruction.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions in Compression Path
steps.instructions: Ensure nothing is between the paddle and detector, including pads, covers, foreign objects, or misaligned accessories.

steps.title: Verify System Ready and Calibration State
steps.instructions: Check system status for readiness and calibration prompts. If available, attempt a user-level calibration or reset sequence.

steps.title: Power Cycle the System
steps.instructions: Perform a full system shutdown and restart following proper procedure.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Paddle (If Available)
steps.instructions: Install a different compatible compression paddle. If the issue resolves, the original paddle is likely faulty. If it persists, the issue is likely system-side.

CCR.Complaint: Compression paddle error displayed and system would not allow proper compression.
CCR.Cause: Compression paddle not fully seated, preventing proper sensor recognition.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated paddle and verified normal operation with successful compression test.

title: Hologic Dimensions Series Mammography System - Biopsy / needle guidance system errors
description: Troubleshooting guide for biopsy or needle guidance system errors on Hologic Dimensions Series mammography systems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no biopsy procedure is in progress and no needle is engaged. If necessary, move the patient to another unit.

steps.title: Observe Error Message and System Status
steps.instructions: Note the exact biopsy or needle guidance error message. Verify the system is fully in biopsy mode and initialized.

steps.title: Verify Biopsy Attachment Installation
steps.instructions: Inspect the biopsy device attachment: fully seated and locked into position, correct accessory for the procedure, no looseness or misalignment. Remove and reseat if needed.

steps.title: Inspect Mechanical Alignment and Mounting Area
steps.instructions: Check for misalignment between biopsy unit and gantry, obstructions or debris in mounting interface, and physical damage to brackets or connectors.

steps.title: Check Cable Connections (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Inspect any visible cables between the biopsy unit and system: secure connections, no bent pins or damage, proper routing without strain. Reseat connections if accessible.

steps.title: Verify System Calibration / Homing Status
steps.instructions: Check if the system requires homing or initialization sequence and calibration confirmation before biopsy use. Perform system homing if available.

steps.title: Confirm Gantry Position and System Readiness
steps.instructions: Ensure gantry is in correct biopsy position and all system interlocks are satisfied (door, compression, etc.).

steps.title: Restart Biopsy Application / System (If Needed)
steps.instructions: Exit biopsy mode and re-enter. If issue persists, perform a controlled system reboot.

CCR.Complaint: Biopsy system would not initialize and displayed guidance system error.
CCR.Cause: Biopsy attachment not fully seated, preventing system recognition.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated biopsy attachment and verified proper system initialization; unit returned to service.

title: Hologic Dimensions Series Mammography System - Software Reconstruction or DICOM / PACS Transfer Failures
description: Identifies external causes of reconstruction delays or PACS transfer failures including network issues, storage limits, workflow interruptions, or system communication problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm no active exam is in progress and all acquired images are saved locally. If needed, move workflow to another unit.

steps.title: Check Reconstruction Status and Queue
steps.instructions: Review the system for studies stuck in reconstruction or processing. Look for error messages or stalled progress indicators.

steps.title: Verify Available System Storage
steps.instructions: Check workstation storage capacity.

steps.title: Confirm Network Connectivity
steps.instructions: Check network status on the workstation: Network icon connected, Ethernet link lights active, Ability to access hospital network (if applicable).

steps.title: Inspect Network Cabling and Ports
steps.instructions: Check all visible Ethernet connections between: Workstation and wall port, System cabinets and switches. Reseat cables and inspect for damage.

steps.title: Verify DICOM Destination Configuration
steps.instructions: Confirm correct PACS destination settings: AE Title, IP address, Port number. Ensure no recent changes were made.

steps.title: Check for PACS or Server Availability Issues
steps.instructions: Confirm PACS system is operational by: Checking with IT or radiology, Verifying other modalities can send images.

steps.title: Restart DICOM/Acquisition Services or Workstation
steps.instructions: If allowed by policy, restart the acquisition workstation or relevant services.

steps.title: Attempt Manual Resend of Studies
steps.instructions: Select affected studies and attempt manual DICOM resend.

CCR.Complaint: Images not reconstructing and failing to send to PACS from Hologic Dimensions system.
CCR.Cause: Network cable partially disconnected causing intermittent communication failure.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated Ethernet connection and verified successful reconstruction and PACS transfer.
